book_title: The Zohar - Key Teachings from the Book of Splendor
theme: Mystical interpretation of Torah - the hidden light
verses: 1. Rabbi Shimon bar Yochai revealed the hidden wisdom that was sealed since ancient days.
2. The Torah has a body, a soul, and a soul within the soul.
3. The body consists of the stories and commandments. The soul is the inner meaning. The soul within the soul is the deepest mystery.
4. Fools see only the garment. The wise see the body beneath. But the truly enlightened see the soul.
5. When the Holy One created the world, He first created Light.
6. This was not the light of sun or moon. This was the Primordial Light—Ohr HaGanuz.
7. God hid this light for the righteous in the World to Come.
8. Where did He hide it? In the Torah. Those who study with pure hearts find glimpses of it.
9. The Hebrew letters are the building blocks of creation. Through them, the world was spoken into being.
10. Each letter contains infinite wisdom. Each word opens worlds.
11. The Tree of Life has ten Sefirot—emanations through which the Infinite reveals Itself.
12. Keter (Crown), Chochmah (Wisdom), Binah (Understanding), Chesed (Loving-kindness), Gevurah (Strength), Tiferet (Beauty), Netzach (Victory), Hod (Splendor), Yesod (Foundation), Malchut (Kingdom).
13. These are not separate gods. They are aspects of the One, like colors in white light.
14. As above, so below. The spiritual realms mirror the physical. What happens in heaven reflects on earth.
15. The soul descends from the highest realms to be clothed in a body.
16. Its mission is to repair (tikkun) what was broken, to restore light to darkness.
17. Every good deed elevates sparks of holiness. Every sin delays the redemption.
18. The Shekhinah—the Divine Presence—is in exile with Her children.
19. When Israel is righteous, the Shekhinah rejoices. When Israel sins, She weeps.
20. The goal of creation is the reunion of the Holy One with His Shekhinah—the wedding of heaven and earth.
21. Study Torah for its own sake. The secrets will be revealed in their time.
22. The Zohar itself is a garment. Within it is fire. Within the fire is light.
23. Do not worship the garment. Seek the light.
24. The end of all wisdom is: there is nothing but God.
25. Ein od milvado—there is none beside Him.

author: Rhonda Byrne
theme: The Law of Attraction - thoughts become things
verses: 1. The Great Secret of Life is the law of attraction.
2. Everything that is coming into your life you are attracting by virtue of the thoughts you are thinking.
3. Your thoughts become things.
4. The law of attraction says like attracts like. You attract what you think about.
5. Whether you are thinking about what you want or what you don't want, the law responds to your dominant thoughts.
6. Your current reality is a result of your past thoughts. Your future reality will be shaped by your present thoughts.
7. Thoughts are magnetic. Every thought has a frequency.
8. When you think a thought, you are broadcasting that frequency out into the Universe.
9. Like a magnet, that frequency attracts similar frequencies back to you.
10. If you want to change anything in your life, change your frequency by changing your thoughts.
11. The creative process has three steps: Ask, Believe, Receive.
12. Ask clearly for what you want. The Universe loves specificity.
13. Believe that it is already yours. Feel the reality of having it now.
14. Receive by feeling good. When you feel good, you are on the frequency of receiving.
15. Gratitude is one of the most powerful emotions for attracting abundance.
16. When you are grateful for what you have, you attract more to be grateful for.
17. Visualization is a powerful tool. See yourself already living the life you want.
18. The Universe does not understand 'don't' or 'not.' It responds to the picture in your mind.
19. If you say 'I don't want debt,' the Universe hears 'debt' and brings more debt.
20. Instead, focus on abundance: 'I am financially free. Money flows to me easily.'
21. You are the creator of your own reality. You have been creating all along, often unconsciously.
22. Now you can create consciously, deliberately, joyfully.
23. There is enough for everyone. The Universe is not limited. Your good does not take from another.
24. You are worthy of all good things. Believe this. Feel this.
25. The Secret is within you. You are the Secret.

book_title: The Lankavatara Sutra - Consciousness is Key
theme: Mind-only doctrine - all phenomena arise from consciousness
verses: 1. The Buddha spoke on the peak of Lanka to the assembly of Bodhisattvas.
2. All that we perceive is merely the projection of our own mind.
3. The external world is like a dream, a mirage, an echo, a reflection in a mirror.
4. Do not seek the source of suffering outside. The source is within.
5. The alaya-vijnana is the storehouse consciousness where all karmic seeds are kept.
6. From this storehouse, the entire world of experience arises.
7. When the storehouse is purified, the world is transformed.
8. The discrimination between self and other is the root of bondage.
9. When this discrimination ceases, liberation is attained.
10. Words are fingers pointing at the moon. Do not mistake the finger for the moon.
11. All teachings are provisional. They are boats to cross the river. Once crossed, the boat is left behind.
12. Nirvana and samsara are not two different places. They are two ways of seeing the same reality.
13. The Tathagata (Buddha) has no teaching to give. Teaching implies duality.
14. Awakening is simply recognizing what has always been present.
15. You are already Buddha. You simply do not see it.
16. The three realms—desire, form, and formless—are all mind-made.
17. Step out of the three realms by stepping out of discriminating thought.
18. The five aggregates—form, feeling, perception, mental formations, consciousness—are empty of inherent existence.
19. Emptiness is not nothingness. It is the absence of fixed, separate selfhood.
20. When you see that all things are empty, compassion naturally arises.
21. For who would harm another when there is no 'other'?
22. The Bodhisattva remains in the world to help all beings awaken.
23. This is the path of wisdom and compassion united.
24. Turn the mind inward. Investigate the source of thought.
25. When you find no source, you find freedom.

book_title: The Three Baskets - Foundation of Buddhist Canon
theme: The three collections of Buddha's teachings - Discipline, Discourses, and Abhidhamma
verses: 1. The Tripitaka (Three Baskets) contains the complete teachings of the Buddha.
2. The first basket is Vinaya Pitaka—the discipline for monks and nuns.
3. 227 rules for monks. 311 rules for nuns. Each rule has a story of why it was established.
4. Discipline is not punishment. It is protection. It creates the conditions for enlightenment.
5. The second basket is Sutta Pitaka—the discourses of the Buddha.
6. Thousands of teachings given to different people in different situations.
7. The Buddha taught according to the capacity of the listener. This is skillful means.
8. The Four Noble Truths: Life involves suffering. Suffering has a cause. Suffering can end. The path leads to its end.
9. The Noble Eightfold Path: Right View, Right Intention, Right Speech, Right Action, Right Livelihood, Right Effort, Right Mindfulness, Right Concentration.
10. The third basket is Abhidhamma Pitaka—the higher teachings on mind and matter.
11. Abhidhamma analyzes reality into its smallest components. Nothing is left unexamined.
12. There are 89 types of consciousness, 52 mental factors, 28 material phenomena.
13. This detailed map of the mind guides meditation and understanding.
14. The three characteristics of existence: impermanence (anicca), suffering (dukkha), non-self (anatta).
15. All conditioned things are impermanent. Whatever arises will cease.
16. Attachment to the impermanent causes suffering.
17. There is no unchanging self. What we call 'I' is a process, not a thing.
18. The Buddha said: 'Be a lamp unto yourself. Work out your own salvation with diligence.'
19. The Sangha (community of monks and nuns) preserves and transmits the teachings.
20. The Triple Gem: Buddha (the Teacher), Dhamma (the Teaching), Sangha (the Community).
21. Taking refuge in the Triple Gem is the foundation of Buddhist practice.
22. The goal is Nibbana—the extinguishing of greed, hatred, and delusion.
23. Nibbana is not annihilation. It is liberation from the cycle of birth and death.
24. The Tripitaka is the map. Walking the path is up to you.
25. May all beings be free from suffering. May all beings find the path.
